summary: DHH shares 30-day sales results for 37signals' self-published book Getting Real, which earned $120,000 on roughly 5,750-10,000 copies. He highlights that this is 12 times more revenue than their traditionally published Defensive Design book, despite fewer units sold. The post makes a strong case for self-publishing by showing that the traditional publishing model would have required writing 12 books to match the same revenue. DHH acknowledges Getting Real may not be typical but urges others with viable ideas to consider self-publishing.
key_points: Getting Real earned 12x more revenue than Defensive Design despite selling fewer copies
Self-publishing fundamentally changes the economics by cutting out the traditional publisher's take
Even 'best seller' status in traditional publishing (5,000 copies) can mean relatively modest revenue
DHH encourages others to self-publish and share their numbers to see if the model is replicable
money_quotes: We would have had to write 12 books through the traditional publishing model to earn this kind of money on the same number of sales.
That's just an entirely different scale and game.
Apparently 5,000 copies count as a "best seller", despite bringing in 1/12th of the Getting Real book.
If you think you have an idea with enough steam to carry through sales of just a thousand units or more, I urge you to consider self-publishing.
key_insight: Self-publishing can generate dramatically more revenue per unit sold than traditional publishing, making it a far better economic model for authors with an existing audience.
